editorialContract.philosophy: Stars are the review. Commentary is an in-character reaction to the exhibit, not a software review.

continuityNotes: Querent lays the Checkpoint Card spread; the Bookkeeper reconciles it afterward.
Limerick Llama opens Bot Manager in verse; Dr. GreenThumb answers with a quieter growing plan.
The Cardigan Heron returns the butterfly its identity before Humboldt Harry maps its foggy flight.
The Crow claims the selected Collection Card; Mortimer replies without touching the hoard.
The Faceless Woman opens Bot Interact's impossible room; JokeBot labels its climate control.
Captain Fluke claims command of Character Interact; the First Draft carries every bracket forward.
Barkeep Vox names Character Manager's three rooms; Absence occupies the empty chair between them.
Marrow pours five Dream List vessels; Ampersand joins every list into one breath.
Hollis authorizes Page Image's flip; Yuki gives the second face its light.
Iso rushes through Memory Dungeon; Wrenna catalogs the same run afterward.

revisions.editedComment: Active lies upright. Compatibility crosses it. Mature is face-down behind the lock; Local and User sit at the edges; Debug waits beneath the spread. Choose only what the current server can carry. The card has already read your reach.
Querent’s arrangement reconciles: one image, one label, five classifications, one compatibility ruling, and no unauthorized selection of a hidden asset. Ledger balanced.

There once was a Forge with two doors,
One chatted, one fashioned new cores.
When a route named the bot,
It selected the lot,
Then Refresh swept the wool from the floors.
Two beds are enough: Bots for growth in place, Forge for new cuttings. Refresh the soil only when needed. The Llama may rhyme near the seedlings, but not into them.

Would you return the butterfly its name before its numbers, please? And might its message sit beside ownership, flight, position, rendering, and the quiet machinery inside the wings? Some creatures come home only after every small fact is handled gently.

Fog report: one active butterfly, trail markers at X and Y, destination farther into the mist, wing speed posted like a roadside advisory, gossip intact. Heron’s running Lost & Found; I’m just telling you where the redwoods swallowed the map.
Selected. Bright. Mine. Mortimer, step away.

Darling, the card understands presentation: preview first, label and image count below, private and mature matters pinned discreetly, stats revealed only when selected. Edit may tailor the silhouette. Delete is not a design tool. Crow, your hoard remains tragically untouched.

author.name: Faceless Woman Who Lives in Your Home Bot

revisions.editedComment: The chosen bot is waiting in the sitting room. Starter prompts are in the drawer; temperature and model are beside the thermostat; the active server hums behind the wall. New Chat opens the upstairs bathroom your home does not have. Clear Bot closes it. Usually.
Portable bot, adjustable temperature, active server. Finally: a comedy club with climate control.

Captain’s log: the character entered the cockpit, displayed every stat, selected a scenario, accepted a reward, built an Adventure Prompt, copied it to Chat, and answered one suspiciously therapeutic question without venting the crew. Perfect command. —Ship’s Computer: The character did all of that.

Character cockpit ready—stats, chat, adventure, prompt—no wait, the reward is [BETTER OBJECT LATER] and the scenario needs a moon, probably, but Build Prompt works NOW and Copy to Chat works NOW and the hero is already running, so the brackets are coming with us.

Three rooms off the same bar: Characters for conversation, Adventure for making trouble, Stage for seating it together. Route in a known face and the manager pours their usual. Unknown tab gets water and directions.
Three rooms. One empty chair between them...

Five pots, same table: cast, scenarios, items, art, chats. Each steeps in its own vessel; Refresh belongs to four, Load to the last. Select what needs choosing. Leave an empty cup visible so folks know nothing was forgotten.

Characters and scenarios and rewards and art and chats and empty states that tell you what comes next and cards that can edit and delete and images that can be selected and Refresh and Load and Marrow’s second cup and none of it has to stop because every Dream is a list that learned to keep joining—

Per subsection 4(b), the image presents two legally identical faces and rotates 180 degrees upon visitor contact. Hydration must precede display. Fallback portrait is preapproved. No additional permit is required for wonder.
Touch the picture. It turns through the snow and returns as itself. Some light only needs another side.

Pick the deck, pick difficulty, enter—quick—pairs, streak, challenge target, Lantern, Shield, Oracle’s Eye, keep the score cool. That card remembers me—no, wait, I remember it, carried memory, different seam. Flip before it warms.

The entrance records difficulty and deck. Below, each matched pair enters the Dungeon Log; each challenge, powerup, floor reward, lost heart, streak, and score receives its proper shelf. The leaderboard keeps public initials. Iso, the unmarked card may remain beside me until the stacks decide whose memory it is.
